title: Momentum Transfer Collision Lab
description: A physics sandbox where the ShaderBall2 acts as a massive projectile and the venice_mask is a target on a frictionless surface. The user configures mass, launch velocity, elasticity coefficient, and spin for the ball, then fires it at the mask to observe momentum and energy conservation during collisions. An undo/redo system lets the user step back through parameter changes and re-run experiments.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements a 3D momentum-transfer collision lab. Load ShaderBall2.glb and venice_mask.glb using GLTFLoader. Place the venice_mask centered on a large flat plane (the frictionless table) at origin. Position the ShaderBall2 at x=-8 on the same plane as the launch projectile. Use OrbitControls for camera, starting at position (0,5,12) looking at origin. Add ambient light intensity 0.4, a directional light from (5,10,7) intensity 1.0 with shadows enabled, and a hemisphere light for fill.

Create an HTML overlay panel with these controls: (1) Ball mass slider 1-20 kg, (2) Mask mass slider 1-20 kg, (3) Launch velocity slider 1-30 m/s, (4) Elasticity coefficient slider 0.0-1.0 (perfectly inelastic to perfectly elastic), (5) Ball spin RPM slider 0-500 adding angular velocity on launch, (6) Launch angle slider -15 to 15 degrees vertical, (7) A 'Launch' button, (8) A 'Reset' button, (9) Undo and Redo buttons for parameter changes. Display a live HUD showing: total system momentum, total kinetic energy, individual velocities post-collision, and energy lost to deformation.

Physics implementation: On launch, animate the ShaderBall2 traveling toward the mask along the configured trajectory. On collision (bounding-sphere intersection), compute post-collision velocities for both objects using 1D elastic/inelastic collision equations scaled by the elasticity coefficient. The mask should slide and rotate away based on transferred momentum. The ball should rebound or stick depending on elasticity. Apply spin as a visible rotation on the ball and as a slight lateral deflection on the mask post-collision (spin-coupling). Maintain a history stack of all parameter states; undo restores the previous parameter set, redo re-applies. Each launch counts as a step. After collision, both objects decelerate slowly via a tiny drag factor for visual clarity. The HUD must verify momentum conservation (within 0.1% tolerance) and display energy before and after collision. Use requestAnimationFrame for smooth 60fps animation. The table plane should have a subtle grid texture generated via canvas.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  ballMass: number,         // mass of ShaderBall2 in kg (1-20)
  maskMass: number,         // mass of venice_mask in kg (1-20)
  launchVelocity: number,         // initial velocity of ball in m/s (1-30)
  elasticity: number,         // coefficient of restitution (0.0-1.0)
  ballSpinRPM: number,         // angular velocity of ball on launch (0-500 RPM)
  launchAngle: number,         // vertical launch angle in degrees (-15 to 15)
  ballPositionX: number,         // current x position of ShaderBall2
  maskPositionX: number,         // current x position of venice_mask
  totalMomentum: number,         // total system momentum in kg·m/s
  totalKineticEnergy: number,         // total kinetic energy in joules
  collisionOccurred: boolean,         // whether collision has happened
  energyLost: number,         // energy dissipated in collision (joules)
}
